title: Secret Language
editorialSummary: Cryptography terms grouped by the era and approach they belong to. Classical (pre-computer, substitution/transposition by hand): Caesar cipher, Vigenere cipher, Atbash, Playfair. Symmetric key (modern, same key to encrypt and decrypt): AES, DES, Blowfish, RC4. Public key / asymmetric (mathematically linked key pairs): RSA, Diffie-Hellman, Elliptic Curve, ElGamal. Hash functions (one-way, no key, fixed-length output): MD5, SHA-256, bcrypt, Whirlpool. False trail: RC4 looks like it could be a public-key system due to its name; bcrypt looks like it could be symmetric encryption (it uses a key) but is canonically a hash/password-hash function.

meta.gptRationale: The broad crypto categories are accessible to many players, but distinguishing hashing from encryption and placing a few named schemes correctly adds moderate challenge.
